title: Post-Quantum Cryptography and Quantum-Resistant Cryptography
summary: News article from Hungary's National Cyber Security Institute (NKI) on post-quantum and quantum-resistant cryptography, published in its IT security news section.

title: IT Security Press Review (week 28)
summary: Weekly IT-security press review published by Hungary's National Cyber Security Institute (NKI) that discusses quantum communication and post-quantum cryptography (PQC). This is a press-digest compilation of external news rather than an original NKI policy position; flagged for human review.

title: IT Security Newsletter / Press Review (week 47)
summary: Press-review newsletter published by Hungary's National Cyber Security Institute (NKI) referencing the need to develop a post-quantum cryptography (PQC) migration plan accounting for existing systems. This is a press-digest compilation rather than an original NKI policy document; flagged for human review.

title: Post-Quantum Cryptography and the QCI Hungary Project
summary: Presentation on post-quantum cryptography and the QCI Hungary project, authored by Janos Mohacsi of KIFU (the Hungarian Governmental Agency for IT Development), the national node of the EU EuroQCI initiative. Domestic and government-linked but a project presentation rather than a formal published document, and not on the trusted-domains list; flagged for human review.

title: 2024. évi LXIX. törvény Magyarország kiberbiztonságáról (Act LXIX of 2024 on Hungary's Cybersecurity)
summary: Hungary's national cybersecurity act (in force 1 January 2025, transposing NIS2) which contains a dedicated Chapter V on post-quantum encryption (A POSZT-KVANTUMTITKOSÍTÁS, sections 51-56) imposing binding obligations on designated organisations to deploy post-quantum cryptography against quantum-computer attacks, establishing certification of PQC providers, and assigning supervisory powers to the Regulated Activities Supervisory Authority (SZTFH). Verified directly from the official kormany.hu PDF text.
